author: vladimir_ea
content:
I assume you are trolling here? in your world, what is the incentive to run a builder? why would anyone bother?
Also, you must be aware that the PBS and MEV has resulted in bigger rewards for proposers https://ethresear.ch/t/is-it-worth-using-mev-boost/19753 ? and therefore more incentive for people to stake?

author: nflaig
content: ~~the link you shared doesn't exist,~~ generally speaking, MEV is a tiny fraction of the total validator rewards today

author: nflaig
content:
>  therefore more incentive for people to stake?
we already have too much ETH staked, so this would be good to reduce this while not hurting solo stakers in the meantime

author: tersec
content:
I won't speak for Potuz here, but
> in your world, what is the incentive to run a builder? why would anyone bother?

is, from certain perspectives, the sort of wrong way around to ask that question

author: vladimir_ea
content: not sure I follow?

author: tersec
content: well it smuggles in certain assumptions

author: vladimir_ea
content: you mean you would rathe rthere were no builders and proposers built their own blocks?

author: tersec
content: I'm not saying any such thing

author: vladimir_ea
content: well i don't follow

author: vladimir_ea
content: pls clarify

author: tersec
content:
Well,
> from certain perspectives

is not necessarily identifying my perspective with any particular such perspective. So asking
> you mean you would ...

doesn't engage with the lack of stated personal preference. But indeed, and here I don't know <@755590043632140352>'s either, but if one were not to believe in MEV as a particularly worthwhile goal to begin with, the question of how to incentivize the thing one might have no particular interest in incentivizing misses the point

author: vladimir_ea
content: hm, i actually have no idea what you are saying.. sorry

author: vladimir_ea
content: what are the 'certain perspectives' and what is the other way round to ask the question if I asked in the wrong way round? thanks

author: tersec
content: Well, for example, https://www.encryptedmempool.org/

author: tersec
content: This group is diverse and has many views, but encrypted mempools, for example, tend to explicitly try to make certain kinds of MEV less profitable

author: tersec
content: That is their purpose

author: vladimir_ea
content: if you have an encrypted mempool, you certainly remove one reason for private order flow. But not all. For example, fast updates and cancellations are another reason. eg. if you want PropAMMs and the like. if.

author: tersec
content: Yes, sure. They're not all against MEV, just certain aspects. The point is that the concern evinced isn't how to best help the business models of some MEV builders

author: vladimir_ea
content: Right. But given ePBS it is recognised that they have a role to play? and there needs to be some incentive to play a role?

author: tersec
content: Ah, now, that is a question on which I'll defer to <@755590043632140352> since it's about his specific proposal

author: vladimir_ea
content: Proposers receive rewards for doing things, I don't suppose there would be as many proposers if there were no rewards. If you remove the rewards for builders (MEV), then it is not clear to me why anyone would run a builder.

author: potuz
content: The point is not to remove MeV from builders. I think you probably misunderstood the design. If anything it's likely that builders profit from such a design. The point is to remove MeV from validators and give part of it to ETH holders

author: potuz
content: At the same time the protocol gets a bunch of things for free: no more commit-reveal, fast inclusion of transactions, no more free option problems, etc

author: potuz
content: And a bunch of complexity is removed

author: vladimir_ea
content:
is this correct: "The builder who commits to burning the largest amount of ETH wins the slot. " 
Doesn't seem to be compatible with "randomly selected builders"? unless this would mean randomly selecting a subset of builders per slot who are then the only ones allowed  to make offers? Why would you want that random selection rather than trying to maximise the burn every slot?

author: potuz
content: No, the first sentence is incorrect and has nothing to do with the design.

author: vladimir_ea
content: ok, and what is the idea behind randonly selecting builders?

author: vladimir_ea
content: this design? https://ethresear.ch/t/mev-burn-a-simple-design/15590

author: vladimir_ea
content: this design still includes payments payments from builders to proposers?

author: vladimir_ea
content: depending on how bids are handled, one possible issue with this is setting the base fee floor "to the top builder base fee observed D seconds (e.g. D = 2) prior to the time honest proposers propose". On relays currently most bidding occurs right at the end.

author: potuz
content: No

author: potuz
content: But in practice the "builder" will be a staked entity like Kraken and it will sell the block to an actual builder just like it is today

author: potuz
content: Before Gloas

author: vladimir_ea
content: oh really = what is meant by 'payload tip' here: "MEV burn can solve the issue by relaxing the requirements on the pre-execution builder balance to cover a maximum upfront payload base fee of M ETH (e.g. M = 32) plus the payload tip."

author: potuz
content: No
